# The generated remote client covers representative list, path, and JSON-body shapes.
env MINA_FX_AUTO_LOAD_ENABLED=false
freeport
exec mina serve --demo --db $WORK/client-remote.db --yes --host 127.0.0.1 --port $PORT --quiet &server&
httpwait -timeout 10s '"status":"ok"' http://127.0.0.1:$PORT/api/health

# Query/list output is raw JSON on stdout.
exec mina client --server http://127.0.0.1:$PORT transactions list --limit 5
stdout '"transactions":\['
stdout '"transaction_id":349'
! stderr .

# A path argument selects the transaction observed in the prior list.
exec mina client --server http://127.0.0.1:$PORT transactions get 349
stdout '"transaction_id":349'
stdout '"records":\['
! stderr .

# JSON bodies pass through for successful and failing writes.
exec mina client --server http://127.0.0.1:$PORT tags create --json '{"fqn":"RemoteCLI"}'
stdout '"fqn":"RemoteCLI"'
stdout '"tag_id"'
! stderr .

! exec mina client --server http://127.0.0.1:$PORT tags create --json '{}'
! stdout .
stderr '"code":"invalid_request"'
stderr '"message"'

kill -INT server
wait server
